goal: Define the current delivery contract for Vityo planning convergence.
description: Scope: docs/plan/repository-delivery-convergence/Requirements.md and the repository planning root. Context: prior planning material existed in multiple planning roots or plan-named files, so maintainers lacked one current Better Plan contract. Target: REQ-001 through REQ-005 define one final planning structure under docs/plan. Design Considerations: use stable requirement labels and keep product implementation completion separate from plan refactoring. Design Value: a single labeled contract keeps later implementation and validation traceable. Constraints & Risks: do not claim product work is complete only because the planning format is current.

acceptance_criteria.text: Requirements.md exists and defines REQ-001 through REQ-005 for the current planning workspace.

commit.message: Define planning convergence requirements

goal: Record source evidence and open-work signals for Vityo.
description: Scope: docs/plan/repository-delivery-convergence/Evidence.md and imported planning-source inventory. Context: old plan files had to be removed as active sources while their useful delivery signals still needed traceability. Target: REQ-001 and REQ-003 are backed by a source inventory and open-work signal ledger. Design Considerations: record source summaries and signal locations instead of copying old plan prose into a second long-lived source. Design Value: reviewers can see what was consolidated without reintroducing old planning roots. Constraints & Risks: extracted signals are evidence for planning status, not proof that the underlying product work is complete.

acceptance_criteria.text: Evidence.md lists imported planning sources and open-work signals derived from current repository files.

commit.message: Record planning source evidence

goal: Map Vityo planning requirements to concrete checks.
description: Scope: docs/plan/repository-delivery-convergence/Validation.md and final validation acceptance criteria. Context: Better Plan requires validation targets before implementation claims. Target: REQ-001 through REQ-005 each have an explicit check. Design Considerations: use manifest validation, search evidence, and source-inventory inspection rather than informal review. Design Value: validation remains repeatable for future agents and maintainers. Constraints & Risks: final validation must not be treated as complete until repository-level searches and manifest validation are run.

acceptance_criteria.text: Validation.md maps every requirement label to a concrete check.

commit.message: Map planning validation checks

goal: Establish the Vityo Better Plan workspace architecture.
description: Scope: docs/plan/README.md, docs/plan/Manifest.json, and docs/plan/repository-delivery-convergence/Architecture.md. Context: the repository needs one machine-readable planning root with plan-local artifacts. Target: REQ-002 and REQ-004 are represented by a Manifest-only root and one dedicated plan directory. Design Considerations: keep root navigation thin, keep execution detail in Checkpoints.json, and organize by repository delivery responsibility and validation target. Design Value: this avoids duplicate plan roots and keeps state-machine validation cheap. Constraints & Risks: do not add marker files or alternate planning directories to identify the workspace.

acceptance_criteria.text: Architecture.md describes the docs/plan workspace boundary and artifact ownership.

commit.message: Establish planning workspace architecture

commit.message: Validate delivery convergence
